build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: A market phase lets the player buy and sell goods at a town with visible prices, current money, cargo hold contents, and remaining capacity. Buying deducts money and adds cargo; selling adds money and removes cargo. Score 0 if no buying/selling exists or if transactions have no effect on state. Score 0.5 at most if prices are shown but transactions do not visibly change money or cargo. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Route planning lets the player choose a destination and path with visible differences in cost, distance, hazard level, or expected profit. The choice commits the caravan to travel and determines what events may occur. Score 0 if travel is a single automatic button with no route choice. Score 0.5 at most if multiple routes are listed but all have identical consequences. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Travel events such as bandits, storms, tolls, spoilage, shortcuts, or merchant encounters occur during transit and visibly alter cargo, money, time, or risk. The player sees the event and its consequence before arriving. Score 0 if no events occur during travel. Score 0.5 at most if events appear as text only with no observable state change. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

An upgrade system lets the player spend money on improvements — extra carts, guards, cold storage, scouts, permits, or better animals — that change future travel capacity, hazard mitigation, or market access. Upgrades persist across trips. Score 0 if no upgrades exist. Score 0.5 at most if upgrades are purchasable but their effect on gameplay is not observable. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects without restarting: title -> town market -> route choice -> travel/events -> arrival/sale -> next turn, cycling until a profit goal triggers success or debt triggers failure. Both end states are reachable and navigable. Score 0 if any transition is broken or the loop cannot complete. Score 0.5 at most if the loop runs but has no win/loss condition. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The trade network contains multiple towns with distinct price profiles — at least four towns where goods have meaningfully different buy/sell values, creating arbitrage opportunities the player can discover. Score 0 if there is only one town or all towns have identical prices. Score 0.5 at most if towns are named differently but prices are decorative or random with no exploitable pattern. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The cargo catalogue offers variety — at least five distinct goods or cargo types with different price spreads, weight, perishability, or risk profiles that create real loading decisions. Score 0 if there is only one tradeable good. Score 0.5 at most if multiple goods exist but all behave identically in price and risk. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Market conditions shift over time — prices change between visits due to demand, scarcity, seasons, events, or player actions, so the optimal trade route is not static. Score 0 if prices never change. Score 0.5 at most if price changes are mentioned in text but not reflected in actual transaction values. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Progression unlocks new content as the caravan grows — new towns on the map, higher-value contracts, riskier routes, premium goods, or advanced upgrades that were previously unavailable. Score 0 if the full game state is available from the start with no growth. Score 0.5 at most if progression is purely numerical (more money) with no new content appearing. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Travel events have variety — at least four distinct event types (bandits, weather, tolls, breakdowns, merchant encounters, shortcuts) with different consequences, so repeated trips do not feel identical. Score 0 if only one event type exists or events never occur. Score 0.5 at most if events are labeled differently but all have the same mechanical effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The map, route lines, town locations, caravan position, and destination options are readable at 1280x720 without overlap. The player can parse where they are, where they can go, and what each route costs at a glance. Score 0 if route information is unreadable or hidden. Score 0.5 at most if information is present but cluttered or overlapping. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Market prices, money, cargo contents, capacity, and town demand are displayed clearly enough for the player to make informed buy/sell decisions without guessing. Score 0 if economic information is missing or unreadable. Score 0.5 at most if numbers are present but lack labels or context. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Player actions produce visible feedback: buying/selling updates cargo and money displays, route selection highlights the chosen path, travel shows the caravan moving or progressing, and events show their consequence on state. Score 0 if actions happen silently with no visual response. Score 0.5 at most if feedback is limited to a text log with no HUD or map changes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The flow connects title screen to gameplay to result screen without dead ends. The player can navigate from market to route to travel to next town without getting stuck or needing to restart. Score 0 if transitions are broken or missing. Score 0.5 at most if navigation works but requires non-obvious actions to proceed. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: map, towns, wagon, cargo, route lines, event cards, and UI belong to a consistent merchant/caravan visual language with a unified palette and style. The screen reads as a trade game, not a spreadsheet. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or relies on unthemed placeholder rectangles for primary elements. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The map feels authored and geographic — towns have distinct markers or crests, roads have character, terrain is suggested, and the trade network reads as a place rather than a graph diagram. Score 0 if the map is only a table of text or bare nodes and lines. Score 0.5 at most if towns are generic dots on a plain background. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Cargo, upgrades, and goods are represented with icons, tokens, or illustrated pieces that have distinct silhouettes — the player can tell spices from timber from cloth at a glance. Score 0 if cargo identity is text-only. Score 0.5 at most if items have icons but they are generic or indistinguishable. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI panels use styled widgets — framed panels, icon-backed readouts, themed buttons, or parchment/merchant styling — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Numeric text uses a custom or styled font. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled default Godot widgets. Score 0.5 at most if some panels are styled but others remain default. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Motion and atmosphere: the caravan moves along routes with animation, market transactions have visual flourish, events appear with card or popup animation, and the map carries ambient life (swaying trees, flickering torches, drifting clouds). Score 0 if all state changes snap instantly with no animation. Score 0.5 at most if only one element animates while everything else is static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
